title: autoanalyze failed when a table has global index, analyze version = 1
description: ## Bug Report Please answer these questions before submitting your issue. Thanks! ### 1. Minimal reproduce step (Required) ```SQL set global tidb_analyze_version = 1; use test; CREATE TABLE t ( a int, b int, c int default 0) PARTITION BY RANGE (a) ( PARTITION p0 VALUES LESS THAN (10), PARTITION p1 VALUES LESS THAN (20), PARTITION p2 VALUES LESS THAN (30), PARTITION p3 VALUES LESS THAN (40)); INSERT INTO t(a, b) values(1, 1), (2, 2), (3, 3), (15, 15), (25, 25), (35, 35); ALTER TABLE t ADD UNIQUE INDEX idx(b); set global tidb_partition_prune_mode = "dynamic"; ``` ### 2. What did you expect to see? (Required) auto analyze run successfully. ### 3. What did you see instead (Required) auto analyze run failed, global index should be handle specifically. ![image](https://github.com/pingcap/tidb/assets/20724552/90df643e-1da0-41b6-86a4-d02ae8b1b230) ### 4. What is your TiDB version? (Required)
poc: CREATE TABLE t ( a int, b int, c int default 0)
ALTER TABLE t ADD UNIQUE INDEX idx(b);
key_bug_features: auto analyze fails with global index and analyze version 1
bug_features_seed: CREATE TABLE t2 ( id INT, name VARCHAR(50), created_at DATETIME DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP, score FLOAT CHECK (score >= 0));
INSERT INTO t2(id, name, score) VALUES (1, 'Alice', 95.5), (2, 'Bob', NULL), (3, 'Charlie', -10);
ALTER TABLE t2 ADD UNIQUE INDEX idx_name(name);
SELECT * FROM t2 WHERE score IS NULL OR score > 90;
